info.name: gong-entrypoints — datacapture
info.description: Triggers for gong-data-capture (recording consent) entry points, exposed by the gong-entrypoints app. Requests are grouped into a folder per entrypoint. Set the active Postman Environment (datacapture.localhost) to point {{baseUrl}} at the running gong-entrypoints app (always localhost:8080). Two downstream services are involved: RecordingConsentApiServer (localhost:7254) for DCP endpoints, and RecordingConsentTasks (localhost:9095) for features, settings, Redis, and consent email endpoints.

item.name: enable-consent — full flow
item.description: SELF-CONTAINED end-to-end flow: list DCPs → assign DCP to user → migrate consent settings → populate Redis. Run the requests top-to-bottom to enable recording consent for a user. Step 1 captures {{dcpId}} from the list response (set manually after reading). Steps 2–4 can then be run in sequence.

request.description: Lists all Data Capture Profiles for company 9001. Read the response to find a DCP ID, then set {{dcpId}} in the collection variable before running step 2.

query.description: Set this from the list-DCPs response (step 1), or override manually.

request.description: Assigns the selected DCP to user 501 (alice). Controls which consent mechanism (jump-page vs. consent-email) is active for her. Requires {{dcpId}} from step 1.
Upserts appuser_consent_settings rows for all eligible users in the recording_consent DB. migrate-all=true processes every user. Required before the consent email interaction flow works.

query.description: Bypass the already-loaded check and force a reload.

request.description: Forces Redis population of DCP jump-page data for company 9001. Run after assigning a DCP and migrating settings so the consent page resolves correctly.

item.name: simulate-consent-email — full flow
item.description: SELF-CONTAINED consent email simulation: seed Redis call details → simulate invitee acceptance. Step 1 generates a fresh callId and captures it in {{consentCallId}}. Step 2 fires the interaction event using that callId. Run top-to-bottom. Prerequisite: run the enable-consent flow first so consent settings and Redis are populated.

exec: // Capture the generated callId so step 2 can reference it.
pm.test('setup seeded Redis and returned a callId', function () {

request.description: Generates a fresh callId (via ConsentDataFaker) and seeds the Redis consent email call entry with a fake meeting title and future start time. The test script parses 'callId=<id>' from the response and stores it in {{consentCallId}}. Run this first, then step 2.

query.description: Auto-filled from step 1. Run step 1 first, or set {{consentCallId}} manually.
ConsentEmailResponse enum — ACCEPTED, DENIED, or NO_RESPONSE.

request.description: Fires a consent interaction event for the call created in step 1. Generates fresh emailId and inviteeId automatically. Simulates the invitee accepting the consent email. Change response=DENIED to test the decline path.
Same as step 2 but simulates the invitee declining consent. Run after step 1 (uses the same {{consentCallId}}).

item.name: dcp-profile
item.description: Data Capture Profile read/list operations. DCP controls which consent mechanism a user is subject to. Downstream: RecordingConsentApiServer (localhost:7254) → TroubleshootingDataCaptureProfile.

request.description: Lists all DCPs for a company. Returns JSON from TroubleshootingDataCaptureProfile.listDataCaptureProfiles().
Returns the company's default DCP.
Assigns a DCP to a user. Requires {{dcpId}} — run list-dcps first.

item.name: consent-features
item.description: Recording consent feature flag management. The only current feature is FOR_TEST. Downstream: RecordingConsentTasks (localhost:9095) → TroubleshooterRecordingConsentFeatures.

request.description: Returns enabled/disabled state for all RecordingConsentFeatureName values.
Enables the specified feature globally.
Disables the specified feature globally.

item.name: consent-settings
item.description: Recording consent settings migration and cleanup. The migrate endpoint seeds appuser_consent_settings rows in the recording_consent DB. Downstream: RecordingConsentTasks (localhost:9095) → TroubleshootingConsentSettings.

request.description: Upserts appuser_consent_settings for all eligible users. Required before the consent email interaction flow works. migrate-all=true processes every user; remove it and set limit=N to batch.
Deletes all consent settings for the company. Use to reset state before re-running migrate, or to test the 'first consent' path from scratch.

item.name: consent-redis
item.description: DCP jump-page Redis management. Downstream: RecordingConsentTasks (localhost:9095) → TroubleshootingDcpJumpPageRedis.

request.description: Forces Redis population of DCP jump-page data for the company. Run after set-dcp and migrate-consent-settings to make the new state visible to the consent page.

item.name: consent-email
item.description: Individual consent email operations: seed Redis call details and simulate interactions. Downstream: RecordingConsentTasks (localhost:9095) → TroubleshootingConsentEmail.

query.description: Leave blank to auto-generate. Supply a value to reuse a specific callId.

request.description: Generates a fresh callId and seeds the Redis consent email call entry. Returns 'Done — seeded Redis call details: callId=<id>'. Prerequisite for send-interaction-event.
Reads the Redis consent email call details for a callId. Use to verify set-call-details wrote correctly.
Simulates invitee accepting consent. Fires the interaction event that drives the consent decision pipeline. emailId and inviteeId are auto-generated by ConsentDataFaker.
Same as above but simulates invitee declining. Use to test the DENIED branch of the pipeline.

variable.description: Set manually after running list-dcps (step 1 of the enable-consent flow). The DCP ID to assign to a user.
Auto-populated by the simulate-consent-email folder's step 1 (set-call-details). The callId used throughout the consent email simulation.
